title: Shri Kaal Bhairav Ashtakam
intro: Read Shri Kaal Bhairav Ashtakam with Hindi meaning. Chant this sacred hymn to seek the blessings of Lord Kaal Bhairav.
description:
|| Shri Kalabhairavastakam ||

1. Devaraja-sevyamana-pavana-anghri-pankajam vyala-yajna-sutram-indu-shekharam kripakaram.
Naradadi-yogivrunda-vanditam digambaram Kashikapuradhinatha-Kalabhairavam bhaje.||
2. Bhanukoti-bhasvaram bhavabdhi-tarakam param Nilakantham-eepsitartha-dayakam Trilochanam.
Kalakalam-ambujaksham-aksha-shulam-aksharam Kashikapuradhinatha-Kalabhairavam bhaje.||
3. Shula-tanka-pasha-danda-panim-adikaranam shyamakayam-adidevam-aksharam niramayam.
Bhimavikramam prabhum vicitra-tandava-priyam Kashikapuradhinatha-Kalabhairavam bhaje.||
4. Bhukti-mukti-dayakam prashasta-charu-vigraham bhaktavatsalam sthitam samastaloka-vigraham.
Vinikvanan-manojna-hema-kinkini-lasat-katim Kashikapuradhinatha-Kalabhairavam bhaje.||
5. Dharma-setu-palakam tvadharma-marga-nashanam karma-pasha-mocakam susharma-dhayakam vibhum.
Swarnavarna-shesha-pasha-shobhitanga-mandalam Kashikapuradhinatha-Kalabhairavam bhaje.||
6. Ratna-paduka-prabhabhirama-pada-yugmakam nityam-advitiyam-ishtadaivatam niranjanam.
Mrityu-darpa-nashanam karala-danshtra-mokshanam Kashikapuradhinatha-Kalabhairavam bhaje.||
7. Attahasa-bhinna-padma-jandakosha-santatim drishtipat-tanashta-papajalam-ugrashasanam.
Ashtasiddhi-dayakam kapala-malika-dharam Kashikapuradhinatha-Kalabhairavam bhaje.||
8. Bhuta-sangha-nayakam vishala-kirti-dayakam Kashivasaloka-punyapapa-shodhakam vibhum.
Niti-marga-kovidam puratanam jagatpatim Kashikapuradhinatha-Kalabhairavam bhaje.||

Phala Shruti:
Kalabhairavastakam pathanti ye manoharam jnanamukti-sadhanam vicitrapunya-vardhanam.
Shoka-moha-dainya-lobha-kopa-tapa-nashanam prayanti Kalabhairavanghri-sannidhim nara dhruvam.||

|| Iti Kalabhairavastakam Sampurnam ||

faq.a1: Kalabhairavastakam is a powerful hymn consisting of eight verses (Ashtakam) composed by Adi Shankaracharya, dedicated to Lord Kalabhairava, a fierce manifestation of Lord Shiva.
faq.a2: Kalabhairava is considered the "Kotwal" (Guardian) of Kashi (Varanasi). He is the Lord of Time and represents the destructive aspect of the divine that clears the path for transformation.
faq.q3: What are the benefits of reciting this hymn?
faq.a3: Recitation helps in overcoming fear, sorrow, greed, anger, and delusion. It is believed to grant wisdom, liberation (Moksha), and protection from negative influences.
faq.q4: Why is it associated with Kashi/Varanasi?
faq.a4: In the spiritual tradition of Varanasi, it is believed that Lord Kalabhairava guards the city and blesses its inhabitants. This stotra pays homage to him specifically as the Lord of Kashi (Kashikapuradhinatha).
